source: 新丝路中级速成商务汉语I (New Silk Road Intermediate Business Chinese I)
notes: Task-based spoken-Chinese textbook, 10 lessons. Primary OCR artefacts: 'PDG'/'DG'/'PDC' watermarks scattered throughout, minor punctuation breaks in dialogue, one truncated dialogue (L4 T1 missing one Wang line). Overall text is highly legible.

model_essays.title: 宴会祝酒辞（欢迎三星代表团）
model_essays.genre: 正式祝酒辞 (formal banquet toast speech)
model_essays.text:
女士们、先生们：
晚上好！今天我们在这里举行宴会欢迎韩国三星公司代表团。首先，我谨代表五星电器集团全体员工对远道而来的客人表示热烈的欢迎！同时也非常感谢各位能出席我们的宴会。
三星公司是我们的老朋友，和我们集团有良好的合作关系，我们的合作一直都很成功，双方都非常满意，也很重视多年的友谊。这次三星公司派代表团来北京与我们洽谈以后的合作计划，我们深感荣幸。希望我们洽谈成功，以后能更好地合作。
现在请大家举杯，为了我们双方合作成功、为了我们的友谊、为了大家的健康，干杯！
model_essays.summary: A complete, formal banquet toast delivered by the CEO of Wuxing Electric welcoming a Korean Samsung delegation to Beijing. Three-part structure: welcome/thanks → relationship recap + purpose of visit → classical quotation + toast.
model_essays.why_study: This is the most complete formal written-register text in the book. It models the exact structure Chinese executives use for business banquet speeches: opening salutation, institutional self-representation with 我谨代表, relationship background, forward-looking wish, then a classical allusion before the toast. Immediately usable at professional events.
key_devices: Opening '女士们、先生们' salutation
我谨代表...对...表示热烈的欢迎 — the standard formal welcome formula
远道而来的客人 — stock polite term for visiting guests
历史回顾 + 展望未来 sandwich structure (past cooperation → future hopes)
引用《论语》'有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎' to elevate register
Parallel tricolon toast: 为了...、为了...、为了...,干杯

model_essays.title: 机场接人（正式会面对话）
model_essays.genre: 商务对话 (business dialogue — airport pickup + formal first meeting)
model_essays.text:
（在机场）
李建明：您好！请问您是美国国际贸易公司的史密斯先生吗?
史密斯：是的。请问您是……
李建明：我是五星电器集团的公关部主任李建明，公司派我来接您。
史密斯：李先生您好，谢谢您来接我。飞机晚点了一个多小时，让您久等了。
李建明：没什么。这是我的名片，您在北京期间有什么问题都可以和我联系。
史密斯：那以后要多麻烦您了。这是我的名片，请多多关照。
李建明：您太客气了。公司的车在停车场等我们，我帮您拿行李，我们走吧。
史密斯：多谢！

（正式见面）
李建明：我来介绍一下儿，这位是美国国际贸易公司的副总裁史密斯先生，这是我们公司的总经理王志东。
（双方握手）
王志东：幸会幸会！史密斯先生，我代表本公司对您的到来表示热烈的欢迎！
史密斯：谢谢王总经理，很高兴认识您。
王志东：请坐！我听说昨天您的飞机晚点了，一路上还顺利吧?
史密斯：飞机晚点了一个多小时，不过一路上还是挺顺利的。
王志东：您是第一次到北京来吗?
史密斯：是的。北京给我的第一印象非常好，我想我会喜欢这个城市的。
王志东：北京有很多名胜古迹，有时间我们会安排您去一些有名的地方看看。
史密斯：非常感谢。
model_essays.summary: Two-scene dialogue: (1) company rep picks up foreign business guest at airport, exchanges cards; (2) formal introduction to CEO, small talk about the trip and impressions of Beijing.
model_essays.why_study: Models the full sequence of a first business encounter in China: identification, self-introduction with title + company, apology for delay, card exchange courtesy phrases, then formal third-party introduction and welcoming small talk. All phrases are immediately deployable.
key_devices: 公司派我来接您 — 'the company sent me to pick you up' (institutional framing)
让您久等了 — stock apology for making someone wait
请多多关照 — indispensable card-exchange closing phrase
我来介绍一下儿 — standard third-party introduction opener
我代表本公司对您的到来表示热烈的欢迎 — semiformal welcoming formula
寒暄话题 (small talk template): trip smoothness, first impressions, sightseeing offer

model_essays.title: 约定与取消约会的电话
model_essays.genre: 商务电话对话 (business phone dialogues — scheduling and cancellation)
model_essays.text:
（对话一：约定见面）
王志东：喂，你好！我是王志东。
史密斯：王总，你好！我是美国国际贸易公司的史密斯。我想约个时间和您见面，您什么时候有空？
王志东：请稍等，我查一下日程表。我这周的日程差不多都安排满了，只有星期五上午有空。
史密斯：星期五我没有什么安排。那我们定在星期五上午怎么样?
王志东：好的。要是您方便的话，9点钟在我办公室见面好吗?
史密斯：能不能改到10点？要是太早的话，我怕堵车。
王志东：好吧，那就这么定了。

（对话二：取消并改期）
张丽：喂，你好！五星电器集团总经理办公室。
史密斯：你好！我是美国国际贸易公司的史密斯，请问王志东总经理在吗?
张丽：史密斯先生你好！王总正在开会，您有什么事情我可以转告他。
史密斯：我和王总约好了明天见面，可我有急事去不了，不得不取消。请转告王总并代我向他表示歉意。
张丽：好的，我一定转告他。
史密斯：另外，麻烦你问一下王总，能不能把见面时间改到下周一?
张丽：我一定把您的意思转告王总，一有消息我就通知您。
史密斯：非常感谢，再见！
张丽：不用客气，再见！
model_essays.summary: Two phone dialogues: (1) Smith calls Wang to schedule a meeting, negotiating day/time; (2) Smith calls to cancel due to emergency, leaves message via secretary to reschedule.
model_essays.why_study: Covers two critical business phone situations in a single study set. The cancellation dialogue is especially valuable: 不得不取消, 代我向他表示歉意, 一有消息我就通知您 are all high-register, immediately usable phrases that HSK 5-6 learners often lack confidence with.
key_devices: 日程差不多都安排满了 — natural way to say 'my schedule is nearly full'
要是...的话 — soft conditional for negotiating alternatives
能不能改到... — polite request to reschedule
不得不取消 — 'cannot but cancel' (formal compulsion)
代我向他表示歉意 — 'please convey my apologies on my behalf'
一有消息我就通知您 — 'as soon as I have news I will inform you' (一...就...)

model_essays.title: 宴会欢迎宾客及祝酒（对话）
model_essays.genre: 商务宴会对话 (business banquet dialogue — welcoming and toasting)
model_essays.text:
王志东：晚上好，史密斯先生，欢迎光临，您能出席今天的宴会，我们深感荣幸。
史密斯：哪里哪里，谢谢您的盛情邀请。
王志东：您太客气了，请入席吧。我们今晚吃中国菜，不知道合不合您的口味?
史密斯：中国菜非常好吃，很合我的口味。
王志东：那再好不过了。这家饭店是北京有名的老字号，菜做得很地道，特别是这儿的烤鸭。
史密斯：太好了，我一直都想吃北京烤鸭，这次可以大饱口福了。
王志东：您要喝点儿什么？白酒还是啤酒？我推荐您尝尝中国的茅台酒。
史密斯：中国的白酒太厉害了，我喝一两杯就要醉了，还是喝啤酒吧。
王志东：各位，今天我们在这里为史密斯先生接风，我谨代表五星电器集团对史密斯先生的到来表示热烈的欢迎！为了我们尊敬的客人、为了我们的友谊、为了大家的健康干杯！
大家：干杯！
model_essays.summary: A complete banquet scene: host welcomes guest, checks dietary preference, introduces the restaurant and signature dish (Peking duck), offers drinks, then delivers an inline toast.
model_essays.why_study: Models Chinese business dining etiquette end-to-end: welcoming at the door, 入席, asking about 口味, recommending food and drinks graciously, and an inline toast. The phrases 深感荣幸, 盛情邀请, 老字号, 地道, 大饱口福 are all high-value banquet register.
key_devices: 深感荣幸 vs 感到荣幸 — 深 intensifier adds formality
哪里哪里 — self-deprecating response to compliment
合...的口味 — 'suits one's taste' (useful beyond food)
老字号 — 'time-honored brand/establishment'
再好不过了 — 'couldn't be better' (再...不过了 superlative)
大饱口福 — idiom: 'to feast to one's heart's content'
还是...吧 — 'I'd better stick with...' (decision after weighing)

model_essays.title: 个人简历式自我介绍
model_essays.genre: 商务自我介绍 (business self-introduction, written narrative style)
model_essays.text: 我叫李成恩，韩国人，1988年毕业于首尔大学中文系，1990年来到中国留学，1994年从北京大学中文系毕业，获得硕士学位，现在在一家韩国贸易公司工作，主要负责中国市场的业务。我的工作业绩不错，最近老板把我提升为部门经理了。
model_essays.summary: A first-person business self-introduction covering nationality, education (with V+于), current role, and a recent professional achievement.
model_essays.why_study: Compact model for written or spoken bio in Chinese professional contexts. V+于 for graduation/birth is essential for formal Chinese CVs and introductions. The sentence combining 负责 + business area is the standard way to describe one's work scope.
key_devices: 毕业于...大学...系 — formal education construction with V+于
获得硕士/博士学位 — standard degree attainment phrasing
主要负责...的业务 — 'primarily responsible for...' (functional scope)
工作业绩 — business performance/achievements (professional register)
把...提升为... — promotion construction with 把

model_essays.title: 公司简介及交通路线说明
model_essays.genre: 商务简介 (company introduction with access directions — semi-formal written notice)
model_essays.text:
本公司成立于1990年，是一家中型电脑软件公司，主要研究、开发各种电脑软件。我们真诚欢迎来自国内外的朋友与我们洽谈、合作。我们公司位于北京市北部的东方大厦，距市中心仅15公里，交通便利，来公司的客户可以选择以下交通路线：
一、自驾车路线
1. 沿北三环路在联想桥向北转到清东路，直行1800米左右，路西即可看到东方大厦。
2. 沿北四环路至安福桥，向南转到清东路，直行300米右手边。
二、公交车路线
1. 乘坐65、320路在东方大厦站下车即到。
2. 乘坐384、735在安福桥站下车，向东走100米，在十字路口右拐。
三、地铁路线
乘坐13号线地铁在知春路站下车，向西走200米左右在花园饭店路口向北拐，步行10分钟即可到达东方大厦。
model_essays.summary: A company website/brochure-style introduction stating founding year, business type, invitation to collaborate, location, and three categories of directions (driving, bus, subway).
model_essays.why_study: Models the standard format for Chinese company introductions: 本公司成立于, business scope, invitation to cooperate, then structured directions using formal connectives (沿, 至, 即). The enumerated 一/二/三 sectioning is a direct imitation device for professional writing.
key_devices: 本公司成立于...年 — standard founding-year formula
主要研究、开发... — comma-linked parallel verbs for business scope
真诚欢迎...与我们洽谈、合作 — invitation to negotiate/cooperate
距市中心仅...公里 — '仅' (only/merely) for minimizing distance
沿...路 / 至...桥 / 即可... — formal directional connectives
步行...分钟即可到达 — concise transport instruction formula

model_essays.title: 中国银行卡的普及（说明文）
model_essays.genre: 说明文 / 社会现象分析 (expository article on a social-economic trend)
model_essays.text: 20世纪80年代中国刚开始发行银行卡的时候，银行卡是一种财富的象征。随着银行卡的普及，现在使用的人越来越多，一个人有几张卡也很正常，银行卡成了日常生活的一部分。据统计，到2005年底，中国各家银行已经发行了9.6亿张银行卡。随着银行卡使用人数的增加，使用存折的人少了。因为和存折相比，银行卡有很多优势。银行卡除了有存折的存款、取款功能外，还可以ATM取款、刷卡消费、网上消费等，这些功能存折都没有。现在个人办理银行卡的手续越来越简单，只要拿着本人证件填一张申请表就可以，所以使用的人越来越多。
model_essays.summary: A short expository passage tracing the rise of bank cards in China from the 1980s to 2005, comparing them to passbooks and explaining why adoption accelerated.
model_essays.why_study: Dense with the patterns used in Chinese analytical writing: 随着...越来越, 据统计, 和...相比, 除了...外, 只要...就. These five patterns together cover a large share of how Chinese business and news writers structure arguments about trends.
key_devices: 随着...的普及/增加/发展,... — 'as X spread/grew, ...' causal-temporal pattern
据统计，到...底，...已经... — citing statistics with 据
和...相比，...有很多优势 — comparison pivot
除了...功能外，还可以... — 'in addition to..., also...'
越来越简单...所以使用的人越来越多 — parallel 越来越 reinforcing causation

model_essays.title: 花园宾馆电话使用说明（正式通知）
model_essays.genre: 正式书面通知 (formal hotel written notice)
model_essays.text:
尊敬的女士/先生：
欢迎您光临花园宾馆！
宾馆每个房间的电话都可以拨打内线、外线，内线电话免费，打外线电话收费。拨打电话的方法如下：
(1) 客房之间通话：
拨打其他客房电话可以直拨分机号，2到9楼的分机号码是在房间号码前加拨0，例如，701房间的电话号码是0701。10楼以上是房间的房号，例如，1021房的电话号码为1021。
(2) 拨打市内电话：
先按2，再按对方电话号码。例如，要拨打的电话是12345678，应该按212345678。
(3) 拨打国内长途：
先按2，再按区号和对方电话号码。
(4) 拨打国际长途：
先按2，再按国际代码
，然后按国家代码、区号和对方电话号码。
model_essays.summary: A formal hotel room notice explaining the internal phone system: free vs. charged calls, and step-by-step dialing instructions for internal, local, domestic long-distance, and international calls.
model_essays.why_study: Models two key formal writing devices: the 尊敬的 salutation and the 如下 + numbered enumeration structure, which appear constantly in Chinese business notices, manuals, and official communications. The 先...再...然后 sequence structure is particularly clean here.
key_devices: 尊敬的女士/先生：欢迎光临 — formal notice salutation
拨打...的方法如下 — '...as follows' enumeration pivot
先按...，再按...，然后按... — formal step-by-step instruction chain
内线/外线 — internal/external line distinction (applicable beyond hotels)
例如 for parenthetical examples within a numbered list

model_essays.title: 时代商场店庆促销公告
model_essays.genre: 商业广告/公告 (commercial promotional announcement)
model_essays.text:
时代商场位于北京市中心，是北京著名的大型百货公司。商场地下2层，地上11层，建筑面积67,000平米，营业面积62,000平米，有500个停车位。商场从1986年成立到现在已有20多年历史，为了迎接本次店庆，本商场将在本月举办大型促销活动。
活动内容：
活动期间所有商品一律8.8折，部分商品6折，另外，消费满100元返20元购物券，活动期间还有更多更好的礼物等着您。
活动时间：6月18-28日
营业时间：9:00—21:00 / 9:00—22:00（周末）
咨询电话：82148130、82148365、82148410
model_essays.summary: A department store anniversary promotional notice: company profile, physical specs, event details (universal discount, cashback vouchers, dates, hours, contact).
model_essays.why_study: Models the standard structure of a Chinese business promotional announcement. The 本商场将在本月举办 phrasing with triple 本 is a compact formal time-referencing technique. 一律 + percentage discount and 消费满...返... (spend X get Y) are patterns repeated across Chinese commercial contexts.
key_devices: 位于...中心，是...著名的... — location + prestige opener
本商场将在本月举办 — triple 本 institutional time-reference
所有商品一律...折 — 'all goods uniformly at X% discount'
消费满...元返...元购物券 — cashback/voucher formula
活动时间/营业时间/咨询电话 — standard notice block fields

grammar_patterns.explanation: 本 replaces 我们的 or 这个 before institutional nouns (company, city, store, month, week) to create formal, written-register self-reference. Sounds bureaucratic/official; extremely common in announcements, reports, letters.
examples: 我代表本公司对您的到来表示热烈的欢迎。
本商场将在本月举办大型促销活动。
本店主要销售各种服装。
我们将在本周五举行酒会庆祝本公司成立20周年。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Formal construction meaning 'to V at/in [institution]'. Most common with 毕业于, 生于, 长于, 产于, 成立于, 位于. Appears in biographies, company profiles, and any written formal context.
examples: 他1988年毕业于首尔大学中文系。
这种咖啡产于巴西。
本公司成立于1990年，位于北京市北部的东方大厦。
他生于北京，长于北京，是土生土长的北京人。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 随着 N 的 V/adj，...越来越...
grammar_patterns.explanation: Expresses concurrent change: 'as X develops/grows, Y increasingly...'. The backbone of trend analysis in Chinese business and journalistic writing.
examples: 随着银行卡的普及，现在使用的人越来越多。
随着经济的发展，人们的收入也增加了。
随着社会的变化，人们的需要也在不断变化。
随着银行卡使用人数的增加，使用存折的人少了。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 和 / 与...相比，...有...优势/特点
grammar_patterns.explanation: Standard comparison pivot: 'compared with X, Y has [advantage/feature]'. Used in business analyses, product comparisons, trend reports.
examples: 和存折相比，银行卡有很多优势。
和那家超市比，这家商店东西要贵10%左右。
和2000年相比，我们公司的员工人数增加了30%。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 除了 A (以外)，还/也 B
grammar_patterns.explanation: 'In addition to A, there is also B.' Additive, not exclusive. Ubiquitous in both spoken and written Chinese for enumerating features, attendees, functions.
examples: 除了王总以外，还有一位副总经理和几位部门经理。
除了负责公司的手机业务，王经理还负责销售工作。
银行卡除了有存折的存款、取款功能外，还可以ATM取款、刷卡消费、网上消费等。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Soft conditional 'if..., then...'; 的话 is optional but common in spoken register. Used for polite negotiation, offering alternatives, expressing concerns.
examples: 要是您方便的话，9点钟在我办公室见面好吗?
要是太早的话，我怕堵车。
要是您去北京出差的话，最好先预订房间。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Step-by-step sequence connector. Formal or neutral register. In written notices often appears as 先...再...然后..., in speech as 先...然后...
examples: 先拨55#号，然后拨0715就可以了。
如果拨外线，您需要先拨零再拨您要的号码。
先按2，再按国际代码
，然后按国家代码、区号和对方电话号码。

grammar_patterns.explanation: 'Couldn't be more [adj]' — superlative with nuance of relief or satisfaction. Used after a positive development is confirmed.
examples: 您喜欢中国菜，那再好不过了。
只要签个字，所有的事情都由我们来做，再简单不过了。
如果能坐在家里办公，那真是再方便不过了。

grammar_patterns.pattern: V + 不了 (potential complement of impossibility)
grammar_patterns.explanation: Cannot do X — potential complement expressing inability (due to circumstances, not skill). Different from 不能/不会: focuses on situational impossibility.
examples: 我有急事去不了。
我们的产品满足不了顾客的要求。
这件事他毕业以后才做得了。

grammar_patterns.explanation: After a positive evaluation, 就是 introduces a single drawback or reservation. Equivalent to 'the only thing is...' or 'except that...'. Very natural in spoken Chinese evaluations.
examples: 这件衬衣还可以，就是稍微有点儿肥。
这家饭店的饭菜做得很地道，就是服务不太好。
我住的地方买东西很方便，就是离公司有点儿远。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 不得不 V (cannot but / have to)
grammar_patterns.explanation: Formal expression of necessity/compulsion. More formal and emphatic than 必须. Implies reluctance or external force.
examples: 我有急事去不了，不得不取消。
飞机晚点了，我们不得不等。
因为堵车，我不得不把见面时间推迟半个小时。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 一 + V, 就... (as soon as)
grammar_patterns.explanation: 'As soon as [event], immediately [result]'. Used for prompt consequence. Common in business contexts for promising fast response.
examples: 一有消息我就通知您。
他一回来，我就转告他。
到时候电话就会响。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 一律 + V / 一律 + discount/rule
grammar_patterns.explanation: 'Without exception; all uniformly'. Formal written register. Appears in announcements, policies, price notices.
examples: 活动期间所有商品一律8.8折。
所有预订将保留至下午六时。
商务汉语教材编写必然涉及较多的专业知识。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 据...统计 / 据...了解 / 据...介绍
grammar_patterns.explanation: Formal evidential: 'according to [source]'. 据统计 for citing statistics; 据了解 for attributed knowledge. Essential for formal reports.
examples: 据统计，到2005年底，中国各家银行已经发行了9.6亿张银行卡。
据了解，这家公司成立于1990年。

grammar_patterns.explanation: 'The method/requirements/situation is as follows:' — standard enumeration pivot in formal Chinese writing. Always followed by a numbered or lettered list.
examples: 拨打电话的方法如下：(1)客房之间通话…
本次招聘要求如下：①大学毕业 ②英语专业…
本套商务汉语系列教材具有如下特点：1.设计理念新…

grammar_patterns.pattern: 顺便 + 问/说/做 (incidentally; while I'm at it)
grammar_patterns.explanation: Softens an additional request or question by framing it as a minor aside. Very useful in professional contexts to ask follow-up questions without seeming demanding.
examples: 顺便问一下，你们饭店有叫早服务吗？
我去上海出差，顺便去看我的朋友。
顺便问一下，出席宴会的还有哪些人?

grammar_patterns.explanation: Colloquial intensifier: 'so [adj] I could die'. The polar opposite of formal 本 register — signals spontaneous, candid speech. Useful to recognize and produce in informal workplace contexts.
examples: 日程安排得很紧，累死了！
昨天加班加到晚上10点还没吃饭，饿死了。
要办的手续太多了，麻烦死了。

expressions.meaning: 'Delighted to meet you' — emphatic form, more formal and warmer than 很高兴认识您

expressions.zh: 代表本公司对您的到来表示热烈的欢迎
expressions.pinyin: dàibiǎo běn gōngsī duì nín de dàolái biǎoshì rèliè de huānyíng
expressions.meaning: Represent this company in warmly welcoming your arrival — the standard formal welcome formula
Please look after me / I am in your care — essential closing phrase when exchanging business cards or meeting new colleagues
Famous scenic and historical sites — stock phrase for suggesting sightseeing to visitors
'Was your journey smooth?' — standard small-talk opener for greeting a guest who just traveled
Sorry to have kept you waiting — used after any delay; more polite than 对不起我来晚了
Schedule is fully booked — natural way to decline or signal limited availability
Cannot but cancel; forced to cancel — implies regret and external necessity

expressions.pinyin: dài wǒ xiàng tā biǎoshì qiànyì
expressions.meaning: Please convey my apologies on my behalf — used when leaving a message via an intermediary

expressions.pinyin: yī yǒu xiāoxi wǒ jiù tōngzhī nín
expressions.meaning: As soon as I have any news I will notify you — reliable closing promise in scheduling negotiations

expressions.zh: 举行晚宴为...接风 / 送行
expressions.meaning: Hold a banquet to welcome sb. arriving from afar (接风) / to see sb. off (送行)
Gracious / generous invitation — used when accepting or thanking for an invitation

expressions.zh: 我谨代表...对...表示热烈的欢迎
expressions.pinyin: wǒ jǐn dàibiǎo ... duì ... biǎoshì rèliè de huānyíng
expressions.meaning: 'I respectfully represent [org] in warmly welcoming [guest]' — the slightly more elevated form with 谨 (respectfully)
'Feel deeply honored' — stronger than 感到荣幸; used in formal speeches and written correspondence
Guests who have come from afar — polite, slightly literary descriptor for visiting guests

expressions.zh: 有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎
expressions.pinyin: yǒu péng zì yuǎnfāng lái, bù yì lè hū
expressions.meaning: 'Is it not a delight to have friends come from afar?' (Confucius, Analects 1.1) — classical allusion used in welcoming speeches to elevate register
Good cooperative relationship — standard descriptor in business speeches and reports
To negotiate; to discuss (business) — more formal than 谈, implies purposeful commercial discussion

expressions.pinyin: yǐhòu néng gèng hǎo de hézuò
expressions.meaning: That we can cooperate even better in the future — optimistic forward-looking closing in speeches
Time-honored brand / long-established business — used to praise a restaurant, brand, or shop with history
Authentic, genuine (of food, dialect, culture) — '地道的北京烤鸭'
To feast to one's heart's content; to have a real treat (food) — enthusiastic idiom expressing food anticipation
To [toasted topic], cheers! — toast structure; typically parallel tricolon: 为了友谊、为了合作、为了健康

expressions.pinyin: zī dìngyú ... rì ... diǎn zài ... jǔxíng ...
expressions.meaning: 'It is hereby scheduled for [date] at [time] at [venue] to hold [event]' — formal written invitation (请柬) opener; 兹 is a classical formal particle
'Your gracious presence is respectfully requested' — closing of a written invitation

expressions.zh: 如不能出席，请赐复为盼
expressions.pinyin: rú bùnéng chūxí, qǐng cì fù wéi pàn
expressions.meaning: 'If unable to attend, please kindly reply' — formal RSVP request on written invitations; 赐复 is classical
Handle/complete procedures [for X] — 办理手续 is the standard phrase for bureaucratic/administrative processes
'Please show your ID / passport' — standard service phrase at hotels, banks, airports
The line is busy (telephone) — used when transferring a call
To pass on a message / relay information to sb. — essential in phone-message contexts
'By the way / while I have you' — softens an additional question so it does not seem like an imposition
Without exception; uniformly — appears in commercial announcements, policy statements, price notices
'Spend X yuan, get Y yuan back [as vouchers]' — standard Chinese promotional formula
'With the development/spread/growth of...' — the most productive single phrase for trend analysis in Chinese
Exchange rate

expressions.zh: 活期存款 / 定期存款
expressions.meaning: Current (demand) deposit / Fixed-term deposit
Spend / pay by card — modern financial register

expressions.zh: 部门经理 / 公关部主任 / 副总裁 / 董事长
expressions.pinyin: bùmén jīnglǐ / gōngguānbù zhǔrèn / fù zǒngcái / dǒngshìzhǎng
expressions.meaning: Department manager / Director of PR dept / Vice president / Chairman of the board — the key title hierarchy
Born and raised locally; native through and through — idiomatic expression for someone who has never left their hometown

essay_takeaways: Use 本 (本公司、本月、本市、本商场) in any formal/institutional writing to replace 我们的 — it immediately elevates register and sounds like native formal Chinese rather than translated English.

The formal banquet toast follows a four-part scaffold: (1) state the occasion, (2) welcome + thank attendees, (3) recap relationship history + state purpose of visit + express hope, (4) quote or allusion + tricolon toast. Memorizing this scaffold lets you produce any welcome speech with pluggable content.

三 as a Confucian allusion: inserting 有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎 before a toast lifts a business speech to a genuinely cultured register — Chinese executives who use it are perceived as educated. Learn it as a single deployable chunk.

For formal company introductions, open with 本公司成立于 [year], 是一家 [type] 公司, 主要 [scope]. Follow with 我们真诚欢迎...与我们洽谈、合作. Then location with 距市中心仅...公里. This three-sentence template appears in hundreds of Chinese company brochures.

The concessive 就是 pattern (还可以，就是有点儿 X) is how Chinese speakers acknowledge a defect without being blunt. Using 但是 for the same thing sounds abrupt and translated. Master 就是 for polished evaluative language.
The V+于 construction (毕业于、成立于、位于、产于) is non-negotiable for written Chinese résumés, company bios, and formal introductions. It signals that the writer has genuine written Chinese literacy.

Build 随着...越来越... into your analytical writing. It is to Chinese trend analysis what 'as X grows, Y increases' is to English — except Chinese readers expect it and its absence makes prose feel immature.
The 如下 enumeration pattern (X如下：(1)...(2)...) is the standard way to introduce lists in formal Chinese documents. Never use a colon alone without this pivot phrase in formal writing.
For appointment negotiations: 我这周的日程差不多都安排满了，只有...有空 is a diplomatic way to show you are busy while still offering a slot. Pair with 要是您方便的话 for extra softness.
Three-tier apology escalation: 对不起 (casual) → 非常抱歉 (sincere) → 代我向他表示歉意 (formal third-party). Know which register each apology phrase belongs to and deploy accordingly.

Tricolon toasts (为了...、为了...、为了...) are expected in Chinese business banquets. Prepare two or three templates: one for welcoming guests, one for project completion, one for farewell. The structure is always 3 items, ending with 大家的健康.
顺便 is a power word for adding requests without seeming demanding. In professional contexts, 顺便问一下 makes any follow-up question sound casual and effortless rather than presumptuous.
